_note: ILLUSTRATIVE brief derived from the demo Samruk AssessmentResult. Institutional language; node ids in metadata only.
headline: Energy-security value offset by corroborated health and climate harms and an unproven transition plan

statements.text: Samruk-Energy provides material energy-security value but carries high, independently corroborated public-health and climate impacts, with stated decarbonisation intent not yet matched by delivery.
Overall assessment: 46/100 (Developing), flat trajectory; the balance of benefit and harm where life-and-health and long-term impacts are engaged is interpretive and flagged for qualified review.
Reliable baseload generation supports national energy security.
Grid-modernisation and renewable pilot projects are underway, indicating early transition capability.
HIGH: Independently verified air pollution near plants poses public-health harm to surrounding communities.
HIGH: High carbon intensity drives climate harm and exposes the company to stranded-asset and transition risk.
MEDIUM: Continued coal dependence risks high-carbon lock-in and resource inefficiency.

sections.title: Contradictions (Claim vs Action vs Independent Evidence)

statements.text: CLAIM (management): commitment to lower carbon intensity. ACTION: generation remains majority coal. INDEPENDENT EVIDENCE: regional emissions and PM2.5 remain high. Gap: stated intent not corroborated by delivery.
As a state-owned enterprise, fiduciary stewardship to citizen-owners is engaged; transparency and accountability indicators are mixed on available evidence.
Communities near plants bear health harm; grid users benefit from reliability; future generations bear emissions burden; citizen-owners face mixed stewardship outcomes.
Long-lived emissions and carbon lock-in transfer a material, partly irreversible burden to future generations; long-term impact score 40/100.
Elevated disclosure-vs-performance gap (0.55): the decarbonisation commitment is currently supported only by management statement, without corroborating action or independent outcome evidence.
1. Publish an audited transition pathway with dated milestones.
2. Accelerate renewables and grid investment to reduce coal dependence.
3. Disclose plant-level health and emissions data.
ev-001 [SAY] Commits to lower carbon intensity and modernise generation (demo) — source: annual_report, confidence: ai-seeded.
ev-002 [DO] Majority of generation remains coal-fired; renewables share low (demo) — source: regulatory_filing, confidence: analyst-reviewed.
ev-003 [SHOW] Regional PM2.5 and CO2 intensity remain high near plants (demo) — source: independent_dataset, confidence: verified.
ev-004 [DO] Grid-modernisation and pilot renewable projects underway (demo) — source: company_disclosure, confidence: ai-seeded.
ev-005 [SHOW] SOE governance/transparency indicators mixed (demo) — source: ngo, confidence: analyst-reviewed.
